steps.title: Track the labor burden
steps.content: Infected ants stop doing their share of colony labor and receive care. Uninfected workers must still sustain the colony while feeding, grooming, and transporting the infected ants.

steps.title: Explain the shorter lives
steps.content: The matching completion explains the shortened lifespans as the result of compensating for infected workers' reduced contributions while caring for them. Choice A invents transmission risk, Choice C introduces predator attacks, and Choice D gives colony-without-parasites data that would not explain the burden in parasitized colonies. The answer is B.
